q: What types of quantities are related in Section 1.2?
a: Linear quantities (s, v, a) and angular quantities ($\theta$, $\omega$, $\alpha$).

q: In rotational motion, how do all particles of an object rotating around the axis of rotation move?
If $s$ is the linear distance of a particle of a rotating object measured along the arc, and $\theta$ is the angle subtended at the origin, what is the relation between $s$ and $\theta$?
In the relation $s = r \theta$, what must $\theta$ be measured in?
For a rotational object, how does the instantaneous angular velocity $\omega$ compare for all the particles comprising the object?
a: It is the same for all particles.
It is zero for particles at the edge.
It is infinite for particles near the axis.

q: What is the relation between the magnitude of the linear velocity $v$ and the angular velocity $\omega$ of a particle?
In the relation $v = r \omega$, what must $\omega$ be measured in?
To which particles of a rotational object does the relation $v = r \omega$ apply?
a: To every particle of the rotational object.
Only to particles at the axis of rotation.
Only to particles at the outermost edge.
Only to particles moving in a straight line.

q: What is the direction of linear velocity relative to the path of a rotating object?
a: Perpendicular to the path, pointing inwards.
Perpendicular to the path, pointing outwards.

q: What is another name for linear velocity in the context of rotational motion?
What happens if the magnitude of the angular velocity of a rotating object changes?
a: The magnitude of linear velocity will also change, and the object will have tangential acceleration.
The object will have only centripetal acceleration.

q: What is the direction of tangential acceleration?
a: Always tangent to the circular path.
Always perpendicular to the circular path, pointing inwards.
Always perpendicular to the circular path, pointing outwards.
Always along the axis of rotation.

q: How does tangential acceleration $a_T$ relate to angular acceleration $\alpha$?
In the equation $a_T = r \alpha$, what must angular acceleration $\alpha$ be measured in?
What does the tangential acceleration of a rotating object measure?
a: How fast the tangential velocity changes.
How fast the angular displacement changes.
How fast the angular velocity changes.

q: According to Table 1.2, what is the relation for displacement ($s$)?
According to Table 1.2, what is the relation for tangential velocity ($v$)?
According to Table 1.2, what is the relation for tangential acceleration ($a_T$)?
What is the direction of the angular velocity vector?
a: Perpendicular to the plane of rotation and along the axis of rotation.
Tangent to the plane of rotation.
Radial, pointing away from the center.

q: How is the direction of angular velocity usually specified?
a: By the direction of linear velocity.
By the direction of centripetal acceleration.

q: When using the right-hand rule for angular velocity, what do the fingers represent?
a: The direction of the angular velocity vector.

q: When using the right-hand rule for angular velocity, what does the thumb represent?
a: The direction of the angular velocity vector.

q: When the angular velocity is increasing, what is the direction of the angular acceleration vector?
a: In the same direction as the angular velocity.
In the opposite direction to the angular velocity.
Always towards the center of rotation.

q: When the angular velocity is decreasing, what is the direction of the angular acceleration vector?
a: In the opposite direction to the angular velocity.
In the same direction as the angular velocity.
Always towards the center of rotation.

q: What does "linear velocity" refer to in the context of Key Words?
a: The rate of change of linear displacement.
The rate of change of angular displacement.
The acceleration along a straight line.
The change in direction of motion.

q: What does "tangential acceleration" refer to in the context of Key Words?
a: The acceleration component tangent to the circular path.
The acceleration component radial to the circular path.
The rate of change of angular velocity.
